circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track designed by Hermann Tilke on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island, has served as the venue for Formula One's season finale since 2009. It represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One circuit, following Bahrain and preceding later additions in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: Albert Park Circuit is a street track that winds around Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, holding an FIA Grade 1 classification. It serves as the venue for the annual Formula One Australian Grand Prix alongside various supporting racing categories, including the Supercars Championship event.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Styria, the 4. 326 km Austrian circuit was originally known as the Österreichring and served as the Austrian Grand Prix venue throughout the 1970s and 1980s. Following a reconstruction and shortening, the track was rebranded as the A1-Ring and returned to hosting the Grand Prix from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital that stretches 6. 003 kilometres, ranking as the fourth-longest venue in Formula One racing. The circuit hosted its first F1 race as the 2016 European Grand Prix, before becoming a permanent fixture on the calendar as the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Bahrain International Circuit, a 5. 412 km facility that opened in 2004, serves as home to the annual Formula One Bahrain Grand Prix, marking the championship's inaugural Middle Eastern venue when it first hosted the race that year. The track, licensed as FIA Grade 1, accommodates various motorsport events including GP2 racing, endurance competitions, and previously hosted Australian V8 Supercars from 2006 to 2010, while featuring multiple configurable layouts.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya near Barcelona spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced mix of lengthy straightaways and diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile test for drivers. The venue holds an FIA Grade 1 license and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: This 7-kilometre Belgian circuit near Stavelot has been a Formula One venue since the sport's inaugural 1950 season and currently hosts the Belgian Grand Prix annually, having staged races consecutively since 1985 with only two exceptions.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, this 4. 361-kilometer track serves as the home of Formula One's Canadian Grand Prix. Beyond its primary role in top-tier motorsport, the circuit has accommodated various racing series including sports car championships and North American racing categories.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Located in Shanghai's Jiading District, the Shanghai International Circuit is renowned for hosting the Chinese Grand Prix, which ran annually from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. The venue has expanded its motorsport calendar to include the Shanghai ePrix beginning in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located in England near Northamptonshire, Silverstone Circuit has served as the permanent home of the British Grand Prix since 1987, though it previously alternated with other venues from 1955 onwards. The track holds the distinction of hosting the inaugural 1950 World Championship race, establishing its significance in Formula One history. Beyond its primary role as a Grand Prix venue, the circuit also accommodates the MotoGP series' British round.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring near Budapest became the first Formula One circuit to host a Grand Prix behind the Iron Curtain when it opened in 1986. Built in just eight months, faster than any other Formula One track, this 4. 381 km venue was constructed outside the city after plans for a Monaco-style street circuit in Budapest's Népliget park were rejected by the government.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing venue constructed in 1922, making it the third purpose-built circuit globally and Europe's oldest on the continent. Home to the Italian Grand Prix since 1949 (with only a 1980 interruption for renovation), the track earned its "Temple of Speed" nickname through its distinctive long straights and fast-flowing corner design.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, a 5. 807 km motorsport venue situated in Mie Prefecture, Japan and operated by Honda Mobilityland, ranks among the world's premier racing destinations. The track primarily hosts Formula One and Japanese Super Formula competitions, drawing a capacity crowd of 155,000 spectators.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course located in Paradise, Nevada that navigates through the famous Las Vegas Boulevard area dotted with major resort hotels and casinos. The track incorporates several of the city's iconic landmarks, such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, as it winds through the urban landscape.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: The 4. 304-kilometre circuit in Mexico City honors two fatal racing drivers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, who died in 1962 and 1971 respectively. The track hosted Formula One races previously until 1992 on an earlier configuration, before returning to the calendar in 2015 with its modern layout.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary layout constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 3. 363-mile circuit with 19 corners and a qualifying average speed of approximately 139 mph. Designed by specialist Formula One circuit architects, the track was purpose-built to host the Miami Grand Prix, which debuted on the championship calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street circuit that winds through the Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts of Monaco, encircling the harbor area. Though officially recognized by its proper name, the track is widely known simply as Monte Carlo due to its extensive coverage of that neighborhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes approximately 35 kilometers west of Amsterdam, the 4. 259-kilometer Zandvoort circuit rejoined Formula One in 2021 to host the reinstated Dutch Grand Prix. The partnership between the track and the sport will conclude after the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit is a Qatari motor racing venue situated north of Doha, featuring a 5. 419 km track layout with a lengthy main straight of over 1 km. Holding an FIA Grade 1 license, the facility employs artificial grass barriers to protect the circuit from sand intrusion in its desert location.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Brazil's Interlagos circuit, a 4. 309 km counterclockwise track in São Paulo, opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate the late Formula 1 driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aircraft accident eight years earlier. The venue hosts major international motorsport events and features an adjacent kart track dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street racing venue in Saudi Arabia positioned along the Red Sea waterfront, hosting the country's Formula One Grand Prix since its debut in 2021 and recently welcoming Formula E racing as well.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit is a street-based racing venue located in Singapore that winds around Marina Bay, incorporating portions of the Downtown Core and Kallang districts.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced layout combining extended straights with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing all aspects of vehicle performance. The venue holds an FIA Grade 1 license and provides seating for approximately 140,700 spectators.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas spans 3. 426 miles and meets FIA Grade 1 standards, hosting multiple major racing series including the Formula One United States Grand Prix alongside MotoGP and world endurance events. Beyond its current calendar, the venue has previously accommodated various international and domestic racing competitions ranging from IndyCar to rallycross.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Thai and British driver Alexander Albon currently races for Williams in Formula One, competing under Thailand's flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso competes for Aston Martin in Formula One, bringing two world championship titles earned with Renault in 2005 and 2006 alongside 32 Grand Prix victories accumulated over 23 seasons. Beyond Formula One, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans 24 Hours victories and the 2018–19 FIA World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to have won both a Formula One world title and a World Endurance Championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli races for Mercedes in Formula One, having claimed five race victories since entering the series in 2025.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas, where he pursues his career in motorsport's premier racing series.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Gabi Bortoleto is a Brazilian driver currently competing in Formula One with Audi, bringing his racing talent to the sport's top championship.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Valtteri Bottas, a Finnish driver, currently races for Cadillac in Formula One after a lengthy career spanning 13 seasons during which he secured ten race victories. He achieved back-to-back runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship while competing for Mercedes in 2019 and 2020.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto, an Argentine racing driver, currently competes in Formula One for the Alpine team.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan has appeared in seven Formula One races between 2024 and 2025 while maintaining a reserve driver role at Haas. Outside of his F1 commitments, he competes in the European Le Mans Series' LMP2 category for Nielsen Racing.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The competitor claimed victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix during his time with AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar, a driver holding French and Algerian nationality, represents France in Formula One while driving for Red Bull Racing.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari who has achieved seven world championship titles, matching Michael Schumacher's record. The accomplished driver holds multiple records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg, a German driver, currently races in Formula One for Audi. Beyond his single-seater commitments, he achieved victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand racing driver currently competing in Formula One with Racing Bulls.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Monaco-born Charles Leclerc drives for Ferrari in Formula One, having accumulated eight race victories over his nine-season career. He finished second in the 2022 drivers' championship while competing for the Italian team.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Danish driver Kevin Magnussen competed in Formula One across two separate periods, from 2014-2020 and again from 2022-2024. Currently, he races in the World Endurance Championship as a factory driver for BMW with WRT, while also taking part-time opportunities in NASCAR's top series with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris represents McLaren in Formula One after clinching the 2025 drivers' championship with the team. The British driver has secured 11 Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The accomplished competitor achieved a notable victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix during his tenure with Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Checo Pérez, a Mexican motorsport competitor, currently drives for Cadillac in Formula One. The experienced driver finished second in the 2023 championship standings during his time at Red Bull and has accumulated six race victories throughout his 15-season career in the sport.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian Formula One driver currently competing for McLaren, having secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his four-season tenure in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo is an Australian driver who spent 14 seasons competing in Formula One between 2011 and 2024, earning the nickname "the Honey Badger" throughout his career. He secured eight Grand Prix victories during his time in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell, a British driver, represents Mercedes in Formula One and has secured seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season tenure in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, where he has secured four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver who raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to endurance racing. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, following earlier stints in IMSA's LMP2 class and single-seater racing.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin in the sport's top single-seater championship.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver who has held reserve roles with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls during his Formula One involvement spanning from 2021 through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen has secured four consecutive world championships between 2021 and 2024 while representing Red Bull Racing, accumulating 71 race victories during his 12-year Formula One tenure.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu represents China as a Formula One driver, currently functioning as a reserve competitor for Cadillac. The racer participated in the sport's top tier from 2022 through 2024, holding the distinction of being the sole Chinese national to have competed at this level.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has competed as Alpine since 2021, when Renault rebranded its Formula One operation to showcase its sports car marque while maintaining works team status until the manufacturer's 2025 departure. Alpine's solitary victory came through Esteban Ocon at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, with the team currently powered by Mercedes engines and fielding Pierre Gasly and Franco Colapinto.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has competed in Formula One through different organizational structures, with its current racing operation managed by AMR GP Limited under the Aston Martin brand designation.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker Ferrari, stands as Formula One's oldest continuously competing team and its most accomplished outfit, having participated in every World Championship season since the sport's inception in 1950. The squad carries the iconic "Prancing Horse" designation, derived from their emblematic logo.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas founded this American-licensed Formula One team in 2014, which competed for the first time in the 2016 season. Leadership of the outfit changed hands in early 2024 when Ayao Komatsu took over as team principal from Guenther Steiner, who had held the position since the team's establishment.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Sauber was a Swiss motorsport constructor founded in 1970 that competed in Formula One from 1993 until 2025, establishing itself as one of the sport's longest-running independent teams. Before its F1 entry, the company achieved notable success in endurance racing, including victory at the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours alongside Mercedes-Benz. Audi acquired the team and its operations in 2024 to serve as the foundation for its own Formula One program.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing Limited is a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its Woking facility in Surrey, operating as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group under Mumtalakat Holding Company's ownership.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has participated in Formula One since 1954 as both a constructor and engine supplier, with the current team based in Brackley and holding a German racing licence. Following a 2020 agreement, the team became jointly owned by Mercedes-Benz, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff from January 2022. The outfit is traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in the 2024 season as part of Red Bull GmbH's two-team operation. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the team operates as Red Bull's second constructor alongside Red Bull Racing.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor based in Faenza that entered the sport in 2024 as Red Bull GmbH's second team. Operating alongside Red Bull Racing with additional facilities in Milton Keynes, the squad represents the energy drink conglomerate's second manufacturer entry in the championship.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing operates as Austria's Formula One representative while headquartered in England, having been established in 2005 under Christian Horner's leadership until his 2025 departure and replacement by Laurent Mekies. The team forms part of Red Bull GmbH's dual Formula One presence alongside Racing Bulls.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor established in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, emerging from the ashes of Williams's previous failed racing venture. Based at a substantial facility in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team currently competes as Atlassian Williams F1 Team and has established itself as a significant force in the sport's engineering and construction landscape.
